content: Cardiometabolic syndrome (metabolic syndrome) is a cluster of interconnected risk factors increasing ASCVD and type 2 diabetes risk. Pathophysiology centers on insulin resistance, visceral adiposity, chronic inflammation, and endothelial dysfunction. Diagnostic criteria (harmonized definition): ≥3 of 5: (1) abdominal obesity (waist circumference ≥102cm men or ≥88cm women; ethnicity-specific cutoffs), (2) triglycerides ≥150 mg/dL or on treatment, (3) HDL <40 mg/dL men or <50 mg/dL women, (4) blood pressure ≥130/85 mmHg or on treatment, (5) fasting glucose ≥100 mg/dL or on treatment. Prevalence ~20-25% globally, increases with age. Associated conditions: NAFLD, PCOS, obstructive sleep apnea, hyperuricemia/gout, prothrombotic state (elevated PAI-1, fibrinogen), proinflammatory state (elevated CRP, IL-6). Risk: 2x CV events, 5x increased diabetes risk.
